# Lumio Go-to-Market Brief

**Author:** lumio_final_strategist (step 10)
**Produced:** 2026-04-24T03:44:01Z

## Launch frame

Lumio is a quiet product launched quietly. No Product Hunt, no LinkedIn manifesto, no "we raised" post. A founder essay on Substack, a three-minute video walkthrough, a hand-shared link to 40 people who read seriously.

## Three-week launch rhythm

- **Week 0**, soft link to 40 readers Ghaisan personally knows. Ask three to write a public one-paragraph take.
- **Week 1**, publish the founder essay "Why I built a reading app that doesn't want your attention". Link in HN Show, Twitter, Discord reading communities.
- **Week 2**, release the 14 day trial, drop a tweet thread of real Atlases from beta users (with permission).
- **Week 3**, reply to everyone who signed up with a personal note asking what they'd improve. Treat every reply as roadmap fuel.

## Positioning guardrails

- Do not claim "AI-powered" anywhere on the landing page. The page already says it.
- Do not promise what we know we cannot keep, especially on the remember promise. Lumio helps, it does not memorize for you.
- Do not compete on feature count. Lumio competes on feel.

## Pricing strategy

- Free tier is generous enough to last a reader 4 weeks without frustration.
- Deep Reader is the keep-or-leave moment at month one.
- Studio is word-of-mouth, not a self-serve target. Team adoption through individual reader love.

## Channels that matter

- Founder's Substack, newsletter plus essay format.
- Selected reading communities, Ness Labs, Curious Computation, Ribbonfarm adjacent.
- Discord servers focused on reading and note-taking, Zettelkasten, Obsidian adjacent.
- Zero paid spend for first 90 days, measure organic retention honestly.

## What to refuse, on purpose

- No growth hacks.
- No referral perks.
- No "invite three friends for a free month".
- No streak extensions.
- No AI-generated social posts to simulate traction.
